description: Uniform active-surface routing (ADR 01081) on macOS: the A2 analogue of active-surface.spec.json. Surface-sensitive steps that omit `surface` act on the most recently active surface (TextEdit, opened via startSurface), an explicit reference switches the active surface for later surface-less steps, and the mixed test proves the most-recently-active rule across the browser and app kinds. TextEdit + the untitled-document launch args mirror app-surfaces-macos.spec.json; the runner needs Accessibility (TCC) granted, which CI images pre-grant.

tests.description: Launch TextEdit, then drive it with ONLY surface-less steps: type into the text view, find the typed content back (elementText matches AXValue on text views), and screenshot — none carry `surface`. Then reference the app explicitly once and follow with another surface-less find to prove the explicit reference persists as the active surface.

The most-recently-active rule across kinds on macOS: goTo makes the browser active, startSurface makes TextEdit active (surface-less type + find hit the app), an explicit engine reference switches back to the browser (the surface-less follow-up stays there), an explicit app reference switches back to TextEdit, and closing the app falls through to the browser for the final surface-less find. Pinned to chrome so the engine-keyword reference matches the context's session.
